en.text: they should encourage the ignorant in their duties
O Arjuna, noblest of the Bhāratas
O descendant of the Vṛṣṇi dynasty
but should rather engage them in selfless action which frees them from absorption in the modes of nature
by means of the five kinds of sacrifices, etc
always conscious of the Spiritual Being
are born of Akṣara, the Imperishable
From food (transformed into semen and blood)
without making any offerings (of that food)
without any desire for the results
being unattached to the fruits of your actions
depended on by him for any purpose
all the living beings of this world
the soul to be superior to the intelligence
the confusion to neglect duties and cultivate knowledge
the intelligence or faculty of decision
The demigods (as limbs of My universal form)
thus established in the nature of cause and effect
It is this desire to enjoy the mundane
in the terrible action of warfare?
of the division of the modes of material nature (the three modes of goodness, passion, and ignorance), and action (the functional demigods, the senses, and the sense objects)
in the functions of the modes (the engagement of the senses with their objects)
(by the senses, impelled) by the modes
within the modes (in the form of the sense objects of sound, touch, form, taste, and smell)
The learned, such as King Janaka and others
and the destroyer of both jñān, knowledge of the self and non-self as delineated in the scriptures, and vijñān, realisation of such knowledge
the senses of action the hands, legs, etc. (see 13.6)
the yoga of action as directed by the scriptures
by the yoga of selfless action
from the bondage of karma, or action and reaction in the mundane plane
What will be the use of
in the consideration of the welfare of the people
teaching of Mine, of selfless action
is not attached to them, not absorbed in them
should not agitate (by trying to induce them to accept knowledge of self and non-self)
to the state of spiritual consciousness beyond material activity
completely deviated from the goal of human life
do You urge me to engage
by the modes or qualities born of material nature
which is the embodiment of sin
It arises from the mode of passion, and from that desire, blind anger is born
the progeny of the world, with sacrifices for Lord Viṣṇu
from all sin (pañcha-sūnā, five types of sin due to five kinds of violence to other living beings)
on one’s own path of duty (according to one’s appropiate position in the varṇāśram or God-centred socio-religious system)
for that ātmārāma, one who delights in the self
for the purpose of such sacrifice, for Him
and the demigods being thus pleased
and the worst enemy of the living being
The learned, the teacher of jñāna-yoga
is superior to such a hypocrite
by their ambiguous meanings, sometimes praising action and sometimes praising wisdom
as selfless duty offered to Lord Viṣṇu
who partake of the remnants of sacrifice to the demigods (universal administrators subordinate to the Lord)
of those engaged in action in the world

ru.text: для мыслителей, склонных постигать отличие духа от материи
